intro: Converting from TypeScript to PHP swaps a client/server runtime layer for a server-side scripting engine explicitly tailored for rapid web request lifecycles. Developers make this shift when deploying to standardized web hosting environments or when building content-heavy MVC applications on frameworks like Laravel. This changes the execution pipeline from an ongoing event loop to a stateless request-response execution cycle that tears down all resources on every page load.

diffs.from: Strict compile-time structural checking with advanced types, erased at runtime.
diffs.to: Gradual typing with nominal type declarations enforced at runtime, supporting union types in modern versions.

diffs.from: Modern JavaScript-based layout utilizing explicit variable keywords and arrow constructs.
diffs.to: C-style scripting syntax requiring sigil character markers (`$`) on variables and arrow operators (`->`) for property access.

diffs.from: Long-lived application heap processes managed via engine garbage collectors.
diffs.to: Stateless request-scoped memory architecture that automatically flushes all allocations once the active page script finishes executing.

diffs.from: Built-in non-blocking asynchronous event loop processing task queues natively.
diffs.to: Synchronous, thread-per-request execution model requiring external tools like Swoole or RoadRunner for asynchronous behaviors.

diffs.from: Basic utility packages requiring external module integration for extended filesystem and database access.
diffs.to: Massive native function set providing direct database drivers, string filters, and web request utilities natively.

examples.title: Class and Type Definitions
examples.description: Declaring standard typed properties inside class constructors.

tips.title: Remember the Variable Sigil Mark
tips.body: Every variable declaration and reference in PHP must begin with a dollar sign symbol (`$`). Forgetting this character is a frequent source of parsing errors for developers transitioning from TypeScript.

tips.title: Distinguish Arrays and Maps
tips.body: TypeScript keeps arrays and object dictionary maps separated. PHP combines both use cases into a single unified type: associative arrays, which use the `=>` arrow syntax to assign key-value pairs.

tips.title: Account for Stateless Execution
tips.body: PHP reboots the entire application state on every incoming web request. Global variables or in-memory caches do not persist between page reloads; use external stores like Redis or databases to maintain state.

tips.title: Use Modern Strict Types
tips.body: Add the declaration line `declare(strict_types=1);` to the absolute top of your PHP files. This prevents implicit type coercions and forces the runtime to reject invalid argument types with fatal errors.
